title: linum platyphyllum subsp. platyphyllum
intro: Linum platyphyllum subsp. platyphyllum, commonly known as the broad-leaved flax or wild flax, is a plant species within the genus Linum, characterized by its broad leaves and tall stems. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain a high concentration of omega-3 fatty acids, linolenic acid, and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Linum platyphyllum subsp. platyphyllum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting skin and hair care, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the seeds can be used as a dietary supplement, and the plant's extracts can be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
